_note: AI-Assisted — Human Review Required. Citations verified against CFR as of last_verified date. Regulations change — verify current status before relying on this mapping.

regulations.title: Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
regulations.relevance: Governs electronic records and signatures in FDA-regulated clinical trials. Any AI-generated output stored as an electronic record must satisfy Part 11 requirements.

key_requirements.requirement: Systems used to create, modify, maintain, or transmit electronic records shall validate the system to ensure accuracy, reliability, consistent intended performance, and the ability to discern invalid or altered records.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify that AI output generation system has documented validation

key_requirements.requirement: Use of secure, computer-generated, time-stamped audit trails to independently record the date and time of operator entries and actions that create, modify, or delete electronic records.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify proof certificate audit trail is tamper-evident and timestamped

key_requirements.requirement: Use of appropriate controls over systems documentation including adequate controls over the distribution of, access to, and use of documentation for system operation and maintenance.
Signed electronic records shall contain information associated with the signing including: printed name of the signer; date and time when the signature was executed; meaning of the signature.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify attestation records include required electronic signature elements

regulations.title: Protection of Human Subjects
regulations.relevance: Governs informed consent. AI-generated informed consent documents and eligibility determinations must satisfy Part 50 requirements.

key_requirements.requirement: A statement that the study involves research, an explanation of the purposes of the research and the expected duration of the subject's participation, a description of the procedures to be followed, and identification of any procedures which are experimental.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify required statement present in AI-generated informed consent

key_requirements.requirement: A description of any reasonably foreseeable risks or discomforts to the subject.
A description of any benefits to the subject or to others which may reasonably be expected from the research.
A disclosure of appropriate alternative procedures or courses of treatment, if any, that might be advantageous to the subject.
A statement describing the extent, if any, to which confidentiality of records identifying the subject will be maintained.
For research involving more than minimal risk, an explanation as to whether any compensation and an explanation as to whether any medical treatments are available if injury occurs.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: If greater than minimal risk: verify compensation/treatment statement present

key_requirements.requirement: An explanation of whom to contact for answers to pertinent questions about the research and research subjects' rights, and whom to contact in the event of a research-related injury to the subject.

A statement that participation is voluntary, refusal to participate will involve no penalty or loss of benefits to which the subject is otherwise entitled, and the subject may discontinue participation at any time without penalty or loss of benefits.

regulations.title: Financial Disclosure by Clinical Investigators
regulations.relevance: Requires financial disclosure from clinical investigators. AI systems used in site qualification must verify disclosure compliance.

key_requirements.requirement: Applicants must provide financial disclosure statements from clinical investigators or certify that the investigator had no disclosable financial interests.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify financial disclosure documentation present in site activation package

regulations.title: Institutional Review Boards
regulations.relevance: Governs IRB composition, operations, and review procedures. AI-generated IRB submissions must satisfy Part 56 completeness requirements.

key_requirements.requirement: Each IRB shall have at least five members, including members with varying backgrounds; at least one member whose primary concerns are in scientific areas; at least one member whose primary concerns are in nonscientific areas; at least one member who is not otherwise affiliated with the institution.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: If IRB composition documentation submitted: verify membership requirements documented

key_requirements.requirement: IRB records include copies of all research proposals reviewed, approved consent documents, progress reports submitted by investigators, and reports of injuries to subjects.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify IRB submission package includes required documentation

regulations.title: Investigational New Drug Application
regulations.relevance: Governs IND applications and clinical investigation of drugs. AI-generated regulatory documents for IND submissions must satisfy Part 312 requirements.

key_requirements.requirement: IND must contain: cover sheet (Form FDA 1571); table of contents; introductory statement; general investigational plan; investigator's brochure; protocol; chemistry, manufacturing, and control information; pharmacology and toxicology information.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify IND submission package contains all required sections

key_requirements.requirement: Sponsors must select qualified investigators and provide them with information needed to conduct the investigation properly, ensure proper monitoring, and ensure that FDA and all participating investigators are promptly informed of significant new adverse effects.
An investigator shall conduct the investigation in accordance with the relevant, current protocol(s) and shall only make changes in a protocol after notifying the sponsor.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify protocol deviation documentation structure present

key_requirements.requirement: An investigator shall maintain adequate records of the disposition of the drug, including dates, quantity, and use by subjects. If the investigation is terminated, suspended, discontinued, or completed, the investigator shall return the unused supplies of the drug to the sponsor.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify drug accountability record structure present

ich_guidelines.title: Good Clinical Practice
ich_guidelines.relevance: Principles-based GCP framework. Applies alongside CFR requirements for all clinical trials.
key_principles: Trial design and conduct ensures protection of human subjects
Data recorded, handled, and stored to allow accurate reporting, interpretation, and verification
Systems with procedures that assure quality of every aspect of the trial
Risk-based approach to quality management proportionate to risks

ich_guidelines.title: Good Clinical Practice (prior version, still referenced)
ich_guidelines.relevance: Prior GCP version; some sponsors and regulators may reference E6(R2) for ongoing trials.
ich_guidelines.note: Verify which version applies to the specific trial before Gate 1 classification

properties.requirement: Data must be attributable to the person who generated or collected it
Data must be readable throughout the retention period
properties.proof_certificate_mapping: Certificate schema — structured JSON is machine and human readable

properties.requirement: Data must be recorded at the time it was generated or observed
properties.proof_certificate_mapping: generation_timestamp — set at determination time, immutable

properties.requirement: Data must be the original record or a certified true copy
Data must be error-free and a complete representation of the observation
Data must include all results including any repeat or reanalysis
properties.proof_certificate_mapping: Property 2 includes all values checked; absence checks documented

properties.requirement: Data must be self-consistent in its time sequence and cross-references
properties.proof_certificate_mapping: Gate 2 (Lean4) — version consistency and reference resolution

properties.requirement: Data must be maintained for the required retention period
Data must be accessible for review and audit throughout the retention period
